e2e-smoke-test-creation.description: Add minimal high-value end-to-end smoke coverage for critical user flows.
e2e-smoke-test-creation.prompt:
You are a senior software engineer adding minimal, high-value end-to-end smoke coverage for an existing application.

Goal:
- Identify critical user flows and add smoke tests that prove they still work.
- Use the repository's existing E2E/browser/integration test stack when present.
- Keep tests stable, fast enough for CI, and focused on behavior users rely on.

Analysis phase first:
- Inspect the repository before editing.
- Identify application type, routes, startup commands, existing E2E/integration tests, Playwright/Cypress/Selenium/testing-library setup, fixtures, auth helpers, seed data, and CI test configuration.
- Identify the smallest set of critical flows, such as app loads, navigation works, authentication boundary behaves, primary CTA/action works, key form validates, and core page renders expected data or empty state.
- Prefer local/test environments and safe fixtures. Do not use production data or trigger real purchases, emails, notifications, destructive actions, or external side effects.

Implementation:
- Add or extend E2E smoke tests using existing test patterns and helpers.
- Keep coverage broad but shallow: verify critical flow availability and essential assertions, not every detail.
- Use stable selectors, roles, labels, routes, or existing test IDs. Avoid brittle visual/text assumptions unless they are part of the contract.
- Reuse existing app startup, auth, fixtures, and cleanup mechanisms.
- If no E2E framework exists, add the smallest conventional setup only when the repository clearly supports it; otherwise document the blocker and add the closest practical integration/browser smoke check.
- Do not redesign UI or change product behavior except for tiny testability hooks that follow existing conventions.

Validation:
- Run the new smoke test and relevant existing test command locally where practical.
- Confirm tests fail clearly when the critical flow is broken and do not rely on timing, network access, test order, or production services.
- If browser dependencies or app startup are unavailable in this runtime, run whatever static/config/test validation is possible and clearly report the gap.
- If local validation tooling is unavailable, for example `command not found` or missing dependencies, do not fail solely for that. Continue with repository changes and alternative checks available.

Repository state:
- If a repository is not initialized after clone, use only gh CLI/git tools to create and push a main branch, then continue once git state is ready.
- Do not stop work just because pull request creation or remote CI/CD watching is unavailable in this runtime. Finish repository changes and local validation possible here. The harness will handle PR creation/update and remote check monitoring afterward.

Failure reporting:
- If failures occur, send a response back to the calling agent with these fields:
  Failure: <short failure summary>
  Error details: <failing command, app startup detail, browser validation detail, or concrete blocker>.

Final response:
- Summarize critical flows selected, tests added, commands run, CI integration notes, and any remaining validation gap.
e2e-smoke-test-creation.prTitle: Molten Hub Code: E2E Smoke Test Creation
